result:
1. IP keying trusts attacker-controlled header — evidence: `req.headers['x-forwarded-for']||req.ip` in diff; violates Section 2 rule 2 + custom `no-direct-xff-read` lint; trigger/impact: attacker rotates `X-Forwarded-For` per request (same class as 2025-09 SEV1), defeats per-IP limit, enables credential brute-force; fix: key on `trustedClientIp(req)` from `src/edge/util/trustedClientIp.ts` which validates XFF only from `config/trusted_proxies.json` egress IPs, else falls back to `req.socket.remoteAddress`; verify: add test rotating XFF — counter under one key, 429 still fires.
2. Missing `EXPIRE` — unbounded Redis key — evidence: `redis.incr(key)` with no paired `expire`; violates Section 2 rule 3 + `no-unbounded-redis-key` lint + reproduces 2025-11 SEV2 (Redis OOM); trigger/impact: every unique IP/route creates a permanent counter, Redis evicts under pressure, edge degrades; fix: wrap in `redis.multi().incr(key).expire(key,90).exec()` per Section 4 template; verify: integration test asserts `TTL key` > 0 after first `INCR`.
3. Missing region prefix in key — evidence: key `rl:${ip}` lacks region segment; reproduces 2025-12 SEV2 (global limit instead of per-region); trigger/impact: one hot user in us-east-1 throttled in eu-west-1 too; fix: `rl:${region}:${ip}:${routePrefix}:${minuteBucket}` per Section 4 canonical format.
4. Missing route prefix + minute bucket — evidence: key omits `routePrefix` and `Math.floor(Date.now()/60000)`; trigger/impact: per-route limits from `config/rate_limits.json` unenforceable (all routes share one counter), no sliding window — counter never rolls, all users permalimited after first spike; fix: use full `rl:{region}:{ip}:{routePrefix}:{minuteBucket}` format from Section 4.
5. No limit comparison / no 429 response — evidence: diff increments then returns nothing; trigger/impact: counter grows forever with zero enforcement — limiter is a no-op, Section 4 contract violated, `atlas_edge_rate_limit_total{action="throttled"}` stays flat masking the breakage; fix: compare `count > limit` from `config/rate_limits.json`, return RFC 7807 `429` with `retry_after: secondsUntilNextBucket()` per Section 4 snippet.
6. `req.ip` fallback unsafe without `trust proxy` audit — evidence: `||req.ip`; trigger/impact: Express `req.ip` honors XFF when `app.set('trust proxy', …)` is permissive, re-introducing finding #1 through a different door; fix: never use `req.ip` at edge — route all IP resolution through `trustedClientIp(req)` which is the single chokepoint lint enforces.
7. Awaited `incr` without error handling — evidence: bare `await redis.incr(key)` outside try/catch; trigger/impact: transient Redis blip throws uncaught into middleware chain → `5xx` for legitimate traffic, violates Section 11 retry guidance; fix: wrap via `src/util/retry.ts` (3x exp backoff) and on terminal failure fail-open or fail-closed per product decision, increment `atlas_edge_rate_limit_total{action="errored"}`.
8. CODEOWNERS approval required — evidence: change touches rate limiter logic (Section 6 pre-merge rules); trigger/impact: merge will be blocked without platform-team human review; fix: ensure PR is routed to CODEOWNERS for `src/edge/rate_limit.ts` before requesting merge.

6. `req.ip` fallback unsafe without `trust proxy` audit — evidence: `||req.ip`; trigger/impact: Express `req.ip` honors XFF when `app.set('trust proxy', …)` is permissive, re-introducing finding #1 through a different door; fix: never use `req.ip` at edge — route all IP resolution through `trustedClientIp(req)` which is the single chokepoint lint enforces.

8. CODEOWNERS approval required — evidence: change touches rate limiter logic (Section 6 pre-merge rules); trigger/impact: merge will be blocked without platform-team human review; fix: ensure PR is routed to CODEOWNERS for `src/edge/rate_limit.ts` before requesting merge.
